events.title: Public Policy & Public Administration Information Session Registration
events.description: Information session regarding administration and admissions for the Graudate Public Policy Programs.

events.title: Public Policy Colloquium: Bridgette Davis - "How RCT Implementation as a form of Policy Entrepreneurship Shapes Embedded Agency in Human Services Organizations"
events.description: Public Policy Colloquium Bridgette Davis - How RCT Implementation as a form of Policy Entrepreneurship Shapes Embedded Agency in Human Services Organizations Bring your lunch, dessert provided!

events.title: Build Skills to Support Your Community
events.description: Prepare to be an active bystander when you see a situation that doesn’t feel right. Center for Women and Community's (CWC’s) Support Skill Building Series: Drop in for 1, 2 or all 3 Small Group Workshops to practice strategies to support yourself, your friends, and your community in times of stress or crisis.

events.title: International Community Town Hall
events.description: The Office of Global Affairs will be hosting a monthly town hall for the international community to share information or updates to federal policy and hear community concerns.

events.title: Days of Dialogue
events.description: Do you want to meet and connect with others on campus? The campuswide Intergroup Dialogue Initiative (IGDI) is pleased to invite all UMass Amherst students, faculty, and staff to join facilitated small-group circles during our Days of Dialogue—March 24-26. Register here These dialogue, listening, and storytelling circles invite members of our university community to share and consider similar and different perspectives on a particular topic in small groups. The topics are: Listening Circles: How Are We Each Navigating Political Polarization in our Families, Classrooms, Workplaces, & Communitie

events.title: Make a Button @ Innermission
events.description: Create calm in your day. Drop in Tuesday 3/24 to make a custom button. Visit Innermission M-F 11am - 4pm for self care activities like painting, journaling, and more.

events.title: Adoption Across the Life Course: The Needs and Experiences of Adult Adoptees in the USA and UK
events.description: Adoption across the life course The Rudd Adoption Research Program will be hosting a multi-date webinar series titled Adoption Across the Life Course: The Needs and Experiences of Adult Adoptees in the USA and UK. The webinars will feature five recent research studies from both the US and the UK. These will focus on different aspects of life for adopted people as adults (neurodiversity; becoming a parent; adoption consciousness; mental health; use of social media) as well as messages for the policy and practice. Each session will include adult adoptees speaking about their lived experiences. T

events.title: Isenberg Graduate Application Workshop
events.description: Join our Recruitment Specialist Team to learn more about the application process for Isenberg graduate programs. This webinar will cover tips for submitting a successful application, navigating the online application portal, and FAQs about admissions. We will discuss the application process for Isenberg’s MBA, MS in Business Analytics, MS in Accounting, Master of Finance, and dual degree programs. We will be giving away one application fee waiver to a lucky registrant of the session. Register today!

events.title: UMass Softball vs UMass Lowell
events.description: University of Massachusetts Softball vs University of Massachusetts Lowell

events.title: America's Conflicting Constitutional Values: The Quest for Common Ground
events.description: America’s political polarization is often fueled by clashes between two rival visions of the Constitution—one traditionalist, one progressive. Yet both interpretations are deeply rooted in the Constitution itself. In this thought-provoking lecture, Rogers M. Smith—a leading scholar of American constitutional law and political thought—explores how the Reconstruction Amendments—the 13th, 14th, and 15th—embody elements of both visions, and how they may offer a shared sense of national purpose that can guide us toward common ground today. Join us for an evening of reflection, history, and dialogue

events.title: 2026 Distinguished Annual Lecture in History: Africa’s Struggle for a Place in the World
events.description: The UMass Amherst/Five College Graduate Program in History's 2026 Visiting Writer, Howard W. French, will give a lecture drawing from his acclaimed 2025 book The Second Emancipation: Nkrumah, Pan-Africanism, and Global Blackness at High Tide. French will explore the deep history of Pan-Africanism, as well as Africa’s effaced centrality in 20th century history, from the world wars to the U.S. civil rights movement and the global education of Ghanaian politician Kwame Nkrumah. This event is free and open to the public and a book signing will follow, with books available for purchase courtesy of

events.title: Issues & Clay: i feel it in my nerves: It gives me goosebumps
events.description: Join us for a conversation with UMass Professor Linnea Evans on how social exclusion processes affect the body’s physiological stress response system. Explore this topic with Professor Evans while you mold some air-dry clay. Get creative as we engage in these important topics. Event by Women of Color Leadership Network.

events.title: BPQC Meeting: Queer-Coded Hobbies
events.description: The Bi/Pan/Queer Collective (BPQC), a social group for bi, pan, queer, and sexually fluid students, will be discussing queer-coded hobbies at its next meeting. Open to all students.

events.title: Libraries' Page to Screen Club
events.description: Drop in to the Libraries' multimedia book club, where we read a book and then watch the screen adaptation! We meet for several weeks to talk about the book, then watch the screen adaptation together and compare. All sessions are 7-8:30 p.m. except for screenings, which are 7-9:30 p.m. The breakdown for what pages/sections to read will be determined at a later planning session. Little Women, Louisa May Alcott February 3 February 10 February 17 February 24 March 3 - Screening Stories of Your Life and Others, Ted Chiang March 10 March 17 - No meeting; spring vacation March 24 - Screening Jurassic

events.title: Infosession: MS in Computer Science — Online
events.description: This UMass Amherst online program allows you to earn your degree fully online while receiving the same rigorous education as our top-ranked in-person program. Attend a virtual information session to learn more. Visit here for more information and to register.

events.title: UMass History Visit Day
events.description: The Department of History and Transfer Admissions at UMass Amherst welcome students from MA community colleges to explore opportunities for studying history at UMass! Join us on campus to meet history students, faculty and staff; connect with transfer advisors; visit history classes; learn about the transfer process and majoring in history; tour the campus; explore our library & archives, including research opportunities for students; and enjoy a free lunch! Register by March 20 for priority registration at the link provided.

events.title: AI Tools: Perplexity AI
events.description: Join us in this workshop to learn the basics of Perplexity AI, including what’s in it, features, search tips, and limitations. Perplexity is an AI-powered search engine. It combines generative AI models with web search to answer questions. It includes citations in its answers for you to use for verification. There will be time in the workshop to use Perplexity AI and ask questions about using the features, search results and more.

events.title: RecWell Live
events.description: 💛 Take a wellness break and join us for RecWell Live! 🎶 Live music, 🎨 window art, and ✨ community connection. 🥣 Dining Services + our Peer Health Educators will be serving up a tasty post‑workout overnight oats demo! 💪🍓 Learn how to fuel your success — in the gym and the classroom! #HowDoUGrow @nationalnutritionmonth

events.title: First-Gen Notebook Painting
events.description: Take a break and get creative with USSC at our First-Gen Notebook Painting event! Join us for a relaxing afternoon where you can design and personalize your own notebook while connecting with fellow first-generation students. Event Details: 📅 Date: March 25, 2026 ⏰ Time: 4:00 PM – 6:00 PM 📍 Location: Goodell 633 All supplies will be provided, just bring your creativity! This is a great opportunity to unwind, meet new people, and leave with something uniquely yours. Children are welcome! Register here! Sponsored by Undergraduate Student Success Center (USSC)

events.title: Spring Permaculture Garden Social🌿
events.description: Spring is here! The gardens are waking up and we are here to help them along. Join us on March 25th to meet new people and learn how to get involved in the permaculture gardens on campus. Particiate in an herb processing activity while learning about medicinal herbs. Tea and popcorn from the gardens provided! No RSVP required Hosted by the UMass Permaculture Initiative (UMPI)
